div1.entryFree:
Close, subst. 1) enclosure: a tree, which grows here in my c. Tim. V, 1, 208.
2) conclusion, end: the setting sun, and music at the c. R2 II, 1, 12.*
3) union: attested by the holy c. of lips, Tw. V, 161. let me be blest to make this happy c. Gentl. V, 4, 117. keep in one consent, congreeing in a full and natural c., like music, H5 I, 2, 182.
4) hostile meeting, grapple, fighting hand to hand: meet in the intestine shock and furious c. of civil butchery, H4A I, 1, 13.
